steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The noun that fills the blank is followed immediately by “the cellular organs that generate energy from light,” an appositive phrase that renames and clarifies it. After this appositive, an em-dash appears before “reshuffled.”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
Appositives must be set off by matching punctuation on both sides.

Since the appositive has an em-dash after it, it must have an em-dash before it.

Commas would create inconsistent punctuation; a semicolon separates independent clauses, not appositives; and no punctuation would leave the appositive unset off.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
